spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Local Network Access split permissions surface (LNA, local-network-access, local-network, loopback-network); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: no obvious gaps.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Copy" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5068298146414592 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on minimum-privilege
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: minimum-privilege covers the happy path of Local Network Access split permissions and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
